title: Traders are cooked.
author: StevenVinyl
selftext:
Just one-shotted a killer strat that changes regimes on the fly.  
  
Openclaw my ass.   
  
The world is on the palm of your hand with AI, why aren't you using it yet?

comments.author: BuildwithPublic
comments.body: Nice build! Are you just running this on crypto only?

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body: Hyperliquid only for now, but an Alpaca integration is coming soon for equities, options, etc...

replies.author: ziggy_jackson
replies.body: Same question haha, what software/AI is this?? I feel clueless right now

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body: it's Cod3x (not Codex) [https://www.cod3x.org/](https://www.cod3x.org/)

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body: "Create me a killer strat that will detect ranging, trending and volatile regimes, build the tasks out of that, make no mistakes."

replies.author: Automatic-Essay2175
replies.body: Lmaaaoooo

replies.author: BezisThings
replies.body: This is a shitpost, right? "Make no mistakes"

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body: No shitpost here, real thing.

replies.author: No-Consequence-1779
replies.body: You don’t know AI can already predict the future?

comments.author: HeyItsYourDad_AMA
comments.body: The level of detail in the post makes me confident everything he says is true

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body: It is

replies.author: humblejoe1
replies.body: See

replies.author: Fun-Reception-6897
replies.body: What are you selling ?

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body: I know bro

comments.author: shajurzi
comments.body: I'll wait for the followup questions on WFA and such. I remember my first excitement thining I found the pot of gold under the rainbow too. If you stick with it you may find an edge. It's definitely more than than a oneshot vibecoded app though.

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body:
It's a fully built out consumer-grade product. (By a team of people) Been around for a while, only the strat is one-shot ;)
Main moat is automating your strategy using a hybrid Algo triggers + AI (LLMs) trading engine.

comments.author: Professional_Poem_25
comments.body: lol such lousy strategy who’s cooking who ?

comments.author: xwang19
comments.body: If you learn some basic statistics concepts, you'll know the profit generated by AI is likely because of randomness. btw i can see no evidence from the attached pic to support your stance.

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body:
Screenshot can't encapsulate the full extent of what the app does. It's just one segment.
Profit generated over short term is randomness, not profit generated by an AI overall, there are firms consistently generating profits with AI over decades.

replies.author: xwang19
replies.body: Those Hedge funds and proprietary trading firm are indeed using "AI"; however, they are using more simpler models like linear regression, decision tree based (xgboost), etc. To my best knowledge, they are using those simpler model because of the extremely low signal-to-noise ratio of financial time series data. The more complex the models are, the more likely they will capture the noise in the data instead of real profiting signals. Current LLM are extremely complex models that engineers don't even completely understand why it works. Maybe language models works well analyzing fundamentals but not daytrading/short-term-trading.

replies.author: No-Consequence-1779
replies.body: yes, on how wild trends are in crypto, training something on historical data seems almost silly.

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body: that is true

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body:
I never said I've used it for short-term or day trading. I know that pre-trained LLMs aren't good yet to make trading decisions on their own. They are fine however in following rules and conditions.   
So with a good enough strategy + live market data + execution guardrails it might just work. It does ;)

  
Also, ML models + LLMs as a decision matrix is a good combo.

replies.author: No-Consequence-1779
replies.body: I had made a mistake in the sense training on how 'you' would trade can make sense, if of course your trading record is excellent.  Probably manufacting trade data may be better.  Still, it appears to be mostly a mathmatical formula, so I think invlolving an llm is not nessecary.

replies.author: No-Consequence-1779
replies.body: what data are you feeding it for eah call - what llm are you using?

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body:
yes L2 orderbook as well, this particular strat is following those indicators, you can mix and master as you'd like, it's all live candlestick data through a HL webhook. 

Strategy + task prompt + live market data are initiated into a run (prev. triggered by HMA cloud flipping bullish or bearish let's say). LLM just makes the decision whether it should go long/short or just wait it out.

replies.author: No-Consequence-1779
replies.body:
I actually added your missing pieces to my script and it seems to accuracy. So far, it detects signals before they show up on the charts. This was possible with the l2 but it was sketchy with the movement.  

I found only one ‘guy’ with software for automated trading charging 6 grand. I believe it’s also just using math - no ai as ai isn’t needed. 

You may want to go that route so you do not have to support a Saas. 

Especially dealing with money, there are so many regulations. 
Legal fees for compliance would probably be a barrier to entry for most.

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body: why would you pay 6k to have it built when this app is live?

replies.author: No-Consequence-1779
replies.body:
I may have been unclear. It’s already built. Searching to put up a link, it appears there are a few more but still require connection to their backend. 

It is a windows desktop application for crypto trading for Coinbase and a couple others using their api. 

Downloading financial software and running at your own risk requires much less or zero regulations. 

Building a Saas , dealing with money , requires a huge expense , just to go live. 

Your Saas goes down during trading and people lose - you cover it or get sued out of business. 

Which means these exchanges and other Saas systems have to be bulletproof and require top level SLA , very expensive infrastructure. 

You already know this, or you have not got this far. 

Desktop application requires terms of use for the Unser to assume all risk.

comments.author: silphotographer
comments.body:
I mean there's nothing crazy about it apart from being able to process so many indicators before making a decision. You got like 5-6+ indicators your "system" is checking. I wonder how hard it is to achieve confluence unless you selectively prioritize certain indicators and others are deprioritized. To get all these indicators aligned wouldn't be easy imho though it's hard to judge for sure without source code. 

Not that you owe us any answers. If it works, who cares? GL OP.

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body:
These are the selected indicators from the strategy, can be more or less, easy to achieve confluence. 

Code is pulling live data from HL candlesticks and calculating everything on each run.

Works, thanks for the sane comment senor.

comments.author: Remarkable_Mess6019
comments.body: Isn't it better to automate this then to wait for an analysis and then act upon that?

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body:
System is already a trading engine, aka this is automated through algo style triggers. In this case, trending tasks get triggered whenever HMA cloud goes bullish/bearish. 
Ranging tasks get triggered whenever RSI above 70 or below 30. They don't just trigger an event like position execution though, they trigger a prompt run of: scan, analyse, decide to execute or not based on strat. 
If market is volatile, tasks are paused.
Easy as ;)

replies.author: Remarkable_Mess6019
replies.body: Oh I see. How is your winrate and drawdown so far?

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body: Not enough data, but for now 60%, with 5% DD

comments.author: No-Consequence-1779
comments.body:
i am new to equities algo trading (alot in crypto).  what is this regime changing?  
I also went down the path of using an llm to try to generate signals on given input.    
  
I realized while going through data science certification training, to give the llm enough information to do its calculations, that regular software has been doing this for decades before llm were available. discounting the basic back prop data training set stuff they were already doing. 

It appears it is all based on math as far as signals, though it is still an estimated guess. I repalced the undertministic guessing on the LLM with straight math and it has worked much better. And, does not require so much data for each call (everyone 30 seconds to 5 minutes.. ) 

What did you figure out?

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body: what do you see brother

comments.author: Istongyu-1017
comments.body: Interesting post. Will monitor this one.
